description.p: 3va is a JavaScript and TypeScript runtime built on QuickJS with capability-based sandboxing, post-quantum cryptography (ML-KEM / ML-DSA), and WebAssembly support.

Every permission — filesystem, network, environment variables, subprocess execution — is denied by default and must be explicitly granted per script. Post-install scripts are blocked unconditionally; there is no configuration that enables them.
3va ships a built-in process supervisor so pm2 and similar tools are not needed. It is MIT licensed and statically linked with no runtime dependencies.
v2.1.1: timers/promises, stream/web, dns, readline (full Node.js compat), 3va create scaffolding, heap snapshot, SLSA Level 2 provenance, automated security audit.
v2.0.0: performance profiling (--prof, .cpuprofile), Node.js compat improvements, worker_threads, cluster emulation, and REPL plugins.
